data.title: LIFT begins drilling at Yellowknife Lithium Project in NWT; Company excited for progress.
data.description: Li-FT Power Ltd. is initiating drilling at the Yellowknife Lithium Project (YLP) located in the Northwest Territories, Canada. The company, listed as LIFT on the TSXV, as LIFFF on the OTCQX, and as WS0 on the Frankfurt exchange, made the announcement on Feb. 17, 2026, from Vancouver, British Columbia. This exploration initiative signifies a significant development in the lithium sector, indicating the company’s commitment to advancing its projects. This project holds promise for the future of lithium extraction and exploration in the region, marking a substantial step forward in the industry.

data.title: GAM Holding appoints Gerhard Lohmann as Group CFO to aid strategic expansion.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the United States has fallen to 4.3%, marking a significant decrease from the previous month's rate of 4.8%. The report also highlights a notable increase in job creation, with over 500,000 new jobs added to the economy in the past month. Additionally, the report shows a boost in consumer spending by 6.2% compared to the previous quarter. These positive trends suggest a strengthening economy and a recovering job market in the country.

data.title: ITC Secure and IronNet merge to form Collective Defence, headquartered in Luxembourg, with offices in US, UK, and Singapore.
data.description: A new company called Collective Defence has been established through the merger of ITC Secure, a prominent cybersecurity services firm and Microsoft Security Solutions Partner in the UK, with IronNet, a pioneering company in collective defence and network detection technology. The company is based in Luxembourg and has offices in the US, UK, and Singapore. Collective Defence aims to address urgent cybersecurity challenges.

data.title: Argo Graphene Solutions appoints Nanomaterials Engineer Sean McAlpine to Board of Directors.
data.description: Sean McAlpine has joined the Board of Directors of Argo Graphene Solutions, a company listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange under the ticker symbol ARGO, on the OTCQB as ARLSF, and on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange as 94Y.

data.title: ExtraHop expands into Saudi Arabia after international growth; gains traction in government, finance, transportation sectors.
data.description: ExtraHop, a prominent player in modern network detection and response (NDR), has revealed its entry into the Saudi Arabian market from its headquarters in Seattle. This milestone comes in the wake of substantial global expansion, marked by significant expansions and client acquisitions in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia Pacific regions. In the Middle East specifically, ExtraHop has experienced noteworthy progress, particularly in vital sectors like government, financial services, and transportation. The company's momentum in the region is rapidly gaining pace, showcasing a burgeoning client base and increased market presence.

data.title: Japanese company SBI Holdings aims to acquire a majority stake in Coinhako, a crypto exchange in Singapore.
data.description: SBI Holdings of Japan has agreed to acquire a majority ownership stake in the Singapore-based cryptocurrency exchange, Coinhako. The agreement was formalized through a letter of intent.

data.title: Sivers Semiconductors launches new Ka-band SATCOM products, advancing phased array terminals.
data.description: Sivers Semiconductors AB, based in KISTA, Sweden, has introduced new Ka-band Beamformer ICs and Panels that enable advanced Phased Array SATCOM Terminals. The company, recognized for its expertise in photonics and wireless technologies, revealed the availability of its Cloudchaser beamforming technology on February 17, 2026. This development marks a significant leap in the industry, showcasing cutting-edge solutions for next-generation satellite communication systems.

data.title: Steak 'N Shake sales surged after accepting Bitcoin payments, funds staff bonuses. Nine months post-launch, same-store sales rose dramatically. Biglari Holdings-owned chain cites "transformative power" of Bitcoin.
data.description: Steak ‘n Shake, a fast food chain owned by Biglari Holdings (NYSE: BH), saw a significant increase in same-store sales after implementing Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) payments. The popular burger chain credited the "transformative power" of Bitcoin for driving this growth. According to the company, same-store sales have surged since the launch of Bitcoin payments nine months ago. By integrating Bitcoin into its operations, Steak ‘n Shake successfully combined its cash-producing business model with the innovative potential of cryptocurrency. This strategic move has led to a substantial boost in sales for the chain.
